title: unoasync
text: unoasync Disable the handling of asynchronous messages by the application. int unoasync(); Copy Parameters None Return Values Table 1. Values returned by unoasync Value Meaning 0 Successful completion -4 The IPC message queue could not be closed and/or removed -5 Asynchronous messages were not enabled Use Unix platforms only (except Tru64 Unix). Library: yrtl . Description unoasync disables the handling of asynchronous messages by the calling application. It closes and removes the IPC message queue, and terminates the handler thread. It is not required to call this function when exiting the application, because that will automatically close and remove the queue and terminate the handler thread. Typically you would not call unoasync directly from ProcScript (although it is possible to do so by using a C signature), but from a routine in your user 3GL code that also contains the call to uasync . Note: Although the unoasync function still works, it is recommended that you not use it. The handling of asynchronous messages has been replaced by an integrated solution, which can be enabled by including the assignment setting of $IPC_ASYNC . Example: uasync and unoasync The following 3GL code defines a handler that passes the message to the application’s receiveMessage trigger, as well as logging an informational message in the syslog file (this particular action is specific to Unix). The application would perform or activate ASYNC_INIT at the start of the application’s receiveMessage trigger. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <syslog.h> int HANDLER (unsigned char* msg, short len) { char buf[500]; strncpy(buf, msg, len); /* Copy the message */ buf[len] = 0; /* Add the zero terminator */ uputames((unsigned char *)buf, len);/* Pass message to ASYNC trigger */ syslog(LOG_INFO, buf); /* Write message to system log file */ return 1; /* Return any value (it is not used) */ } int ASYNC_INIT () { return uasync(HANDLER); } int ASYNC_DEINIT () { return unoasync(); } Copy Related Links uasync Initialize the Uniface application to receive asynchronous messages. $IPC_ASYNC Create a thread that listens for interprocess communication (IPC) messages.
